ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general toast entry (FOODON_00002473). No specific entry exists for gourmet toast with burnt caramel, saffron thyme cream, and almonds. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A000E covers bread and bakery products broadly. No specific code exists for this gourmet toast variant with cream and nut toppings. Closest parent category used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for this specific gourmet toast preparation.

serving.description: 1 slice of gourmet toast with toppings
serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is approximately one thick slice of toasted bread (approximately 150 g) topped with burnt caramel sauce, saffron thyme cream, and sliced almonds, as typically served at a café.

nutrient_source.title: Bread, white, commercially prepared (toasted) — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy; supplemented with cream and almond component estimates from USDA FoodData Central Foundation Foods
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry exists for Solid Gold Toast as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated by component modelling: toasted white bread base (USDA SR Legacy #18023), heavy cream (USDA FDC #170859), almonds (USDA FDC #170567), and caramel sauce (USDA FDC #169640). Proportions estimated for a 150 g serving (approx. 80 g bread, 35 g cream, 20 g caramel, 15 g almonds). Values expressed per 100 g of the composite dish.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 — white bread GI reference; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC (2008) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Solid Gold Toast as a composite dish. GI estimated at 70 based on the dominant carbohydrate source being toasted white bread (GI ~70–73 per University of Sydney GI Database for white toast bread). The caramel and cream toppings contribute fat and sugar which may modestly lower the effective GI, but the bread base dominates the glycemic response. GI 70 is used as a conservative estimate anchored to white toast bread from the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (70 × 63.75) / 100 ≈ 44.6, where carbohydrate per 150 g serving = 42.5 × 1.5 = 63.75 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from bread and caramel components
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size as one 150 g serving provides approximately 315 mg sodium, contributing around 14% of the 2,300 mg daily sodium limit recommended by clinical guidelines.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (GI 70) and high sugar content from burnt caramel and cream
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit this dish due to its high GI of 70 and a glycemic load of approximately 44.6 per serving, which will produce a significant postprandial blood glucose rise; if consumed, pairing with a protein or fibre source and monitoring blood glucose response is strongly advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from heavy cream and refined carbohydrate from caramel and white bread
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume this dish only occasionally, as one serving provides approximately 9.75 g of saturated fat (nearly 50% of the 20 g daily limit) and a high glycemic load that may contribute to dyslipidaemia and endothelial stress.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Solid Gold Toast is a premium gourmet toast dish served at To;asta on Circular Road in Singapore, tagged under Japanese-inspired café cuisine. The dish features a thick slice of artisan bread that is toasted to a golden finish and topped with a rich burnt caramel sauce, a fragrant saffron and thyme-infused cream, and a garnish of sliced or slivered almonds. The combination of the slightly bitter, deep caramel with the floral, herbal saffron thyme cream and the crunch of almonds creates a complex flavour profile that is simultaneously sweet, savoury, and aromatic. The dish is classified under grains and staples due to its bread base, but its caloric density is elevated by the cream and caramel toppings, yielding approximately 320 kcal per 100 g. The high glycemic index (estimated GI 70) and substantial sugar content (18 g per 100 g) make this a high-concern food for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or insulin resistance. The saturated fat contribution from the cream (approximately 6.5 g per 100 g) is also relevant for cardiovascular risk management. Despite these concerns, the almonds contribute beneficial monounsaturated fats, vitamin E, and magnesium. This dish is best consumed as an occasional indulgence rather than a regular dietary staple, and portion control is advised for individuals with metabolic conditions.
common_user_queries: Can diabetics eat Solid Gold Toast?
What is in Solid Gold Toast at To;asta?

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional entry exists for Solid Gold Toast in any approved primary or secondary source. Nutrient values are estimated by component modelling using USDA FoodData Central entries for toasted white bread, heavy cream, caramel sauce, and almonds. GI is estimated by analogy to white toast bread from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is low due to the composite estimation approach and the absence of any direct measurement for this specific dish.
notes: Solid Gold Toast is a proprietary menu item served at To;asta - Circular Road, Singapore (field survey listing). The cuisine tag 'Japanese' in the input reflects the restaurant's broader cuisine category on field survey, but the dish itself is a Japanese-inspired fusion café toast rather than a traditional Japanese dish. Given the 'Singapore (Japanese-inspired)' region of origin, multilingual names for Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi have been added using descriptive translations of 'Golden Toast' or 'Caramel Saffron Almond Toast' to comply with G6. The Japanese local name ソリッドゴールドトースト is a phonetic transliteration. Nutrient values are component-estimated; a direct laboratory analysis or HPB Singapore Foodo entry would supersede these estimates if available. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA component sources used for estimation. Selenium is -1 as it is not reliably reported across the component sources used. All other -1 fields represent nutrients not reported in the applicable USDA FoodData Central entries used for component estimation. The glycemic load per serving is calculated as (GI 70 × 63.75 g carbohydrate per 150 g serving) / 100 = 44.6. The cuisine_tags 'Pasta' and 'Udon' from the input are field survey restaurant-level tags and do not describe this specific dish; they are not carried forward into this record.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Initial draft constructed with component-estimated nutrients, GI anchored to University of Sydney GI Database via white toast bread analogy, Japanese local name added per G6, ontology codes set to closest_parent with gap notes, all -1 sentinel values documented in notes, aliases_by_language and flat aliases verified for consistency. This iteration failed G6, Q1, Q8 due to missing Singaporean language coverage (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) triggered by 'region_of_origin: Singapore (Japanese-inspired)'.

Iteration 2: Addressed G6, Q1, Q8 failures by researching and adding local names for Chinese (simplified), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi to `local_names` and `aliases_by_language`. Ensured all new local names and descriptive translations were added to the flat `aliases` list for consistency. Verified GI category threshold (GI 70 = 'high' per ≥70 rule), confirmed all aliases_by_language strings appear verbatim in flat aliases, confirmed zero-macro justification not needed (no core macro is zero), confirmed all -1 fields documented in notes, confirmed match_type values are from permitted set only. All hard gates and Q criteria now pass.
